original_hypothesis.title: RollupCreator.setTemplates lacks validation of template bytecode / interface compliance, enabling deployment of broken rollups if owner misconfigures
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setTemplates() allows the owner to set arbitrary addresses for critical templates (bridgeCreator, OSP, challengeManagerTemplate, rollupAdminLogic, rollupUserLogic, upgradeExecutorLogic, validatorWalletCreator, l2FactoriesDeployer) without verifying code size or interface compliance. If mis-set (or owner compromised), newly created rollups can be deployed with non-contract addresses or incompatible logic, leading to bricked systems or fund loss during staking/challenges.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While this is owner-gated (LOW realism under threat model), it is a high-impact configuration footgun: no on-chain guardrails prevent deploying irrecoverably broken rollups. This is practical because template updates are routine operations.
deployment_assumptions: RollupCreator owner can be compromised or can make operational mistakes.
Users/automation rely on RollupCreator to deploy production rollups.
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: RollupCreator.setTemplates assigns addresses directly with no Address.isContract checks or ERC165/interface probes.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setTemplates(...) stores addresses with no validation (no code.length checks, no interface checks)

preconditions: Templates are changed to invalid or malicious addresses

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: deployment of bricked or malicious rollups via factory
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no Address.isContract; no staticcall to expected selectors; no sanity checks on immutables like maxDataSize

analysis_questions: Are there off-chain operational controls ensuring templates are correct (multisig processes, audits, allowlists)?
Would a misconfigured template cause partial deployment with funds stuck (e.g., retryable funding sent)?
Should RollupCreator enforce interface checks (e.g., supportsInterface, codehash allowlist)?
validation_requirements: Simulate setTemplates to EOA/incorrect contract and run createRollup to see failure modes and whether ETH/ERC20 can get stuck mid-transaction
Check deployment scripts for any template validation (none visible)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: If owner key is compromised, attacker sets rollupUserLogic to malicious implementation and creates rollups that steal stakes or bypass challenge logic. Alternatively, accidental misconfiguration bricks rollup deployments.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: High for newly deployed rollups (stakes, fees, and bridge assets could be at risk)
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: Users/validators of rollups deployed after misconfiguration
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Recovery depends on upgrade controls in deployed rollup; may be nontrivial if core wiring is wrong

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing negative tests for invalid template addresses

technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: admin tx then factory deployment tx

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
